    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_message_2">Once granted, the additional settings will be enabled when a display is connected.</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_pref_description">Use Taskbar to launch windowed apps on an external display, similar to Samsung DeX and other solutions.\n\nRequires either a USB-to-HDMI adapter, or a lapdock, and a device that supports video output.</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_pref_description">Click here for info on how to enable</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_help"><b>About desktop mode</b>\n\nTaskbar supports Android 10\'s built-in desktop mode functionality. You can connect your compatible Android 10+ device to an external display and run apps in resizable windows, with Taskbar\'s interface running on your external display and your existing launcher still running on your phone.\n\nDesktop mode requires a USB-to-HDMI adapter (or a lapdock), and a compatible device that supports video output. Additionally, certain settings require granting a special permission via adb.\n\nTo get started, tick the checkbox for \"Desktop mode.\" The app will guide you through the one-time setup process, which involves:\n\n&#8226; Enabling the \"Enable freeform windows\" and \"Force desktop mode\" developer options\n\n&#8226; Setting Taskbar as your default home app\n\n&#8226; Rebooting your device\n\n<b>Why does Taskbar need to be set as my default home app for desktop mode to work?</b>\n\nBy design, Android will only allow the default home app to act as the launcher for desktop mode on external displays.  If the currently set home app does not specifically declare support for desktop mode, then Android will fall back to using a predefined desktop mode interface.\n\nTaskbar is able to work around this limitation by acting as a shim between the system and your preferred launcher.  When Taskbar is set as your home app, when the home button is pressed it will immediately start the launcher you prefer to use on your phone, then quit.  This launcher is set within Taskbar\'s desktop mode preferences by clicking on the \"Primary launcher\" setting.\n\nThere are a couple of caveats to keep in mind when Taskbar is set as your home app:\n\n&#8226; Most app shortcuts (seen when long-pressing an app icon) will no longer be available to your primary launcher.  This is due to an Android system limitation where only the default home app has access to app shortcuts that are dynamically created.\n\n&#8226; Certain animations that occur when pressing the home button may look incorrect.  This is due to the aforementioned behavior where Taskbar acts as a shim when the home button is pressed.\n\n<b>How do I know if my device supports video output to external displays?</b>\n\nThe folks over at NexDock maintain a list of Android 10+ smartphones that are compatible with USB-to-HDMI video output (this list excludes Samsung and Huawei devices).  You can view this list here: http://nexdock.com/lapdock-for-smartphones\n\nGoogle Pixel devices, notably, do not include support for video output.  However, you can still use Taskbar with standard screen mirroring on these devices, either via Chromecast or by using a DisplayLink dock coupled with the DisplayLink Presenter app.\n\n<b>Should I use Taskbar\'s desktop mode over my device\'s built-in desktop mode solution, such as Samsung DeX?</b>\n\nSome OEMs, such as Samsung and Huawei, have implemented their own version of desktop mode, building on top of Google\'s implementation in Android 10.  These solutions have better integration with the rest of the system and are generally more polished overall.  There is no need to switch over to Taskbar if you\'re happy with your device\'s existing desktop mode solution.\n\nTaskbar\'s desktop mode support is geared towards devices that don\'t offer a built-in desktop mode solution beyond what Google ships as stock with Android 10.\n\n<b>I can\'t find the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options &#8211; can I still use desktop mode?</b>\n\nSome devices (such as the ASUS ROG Phone 2) do not have the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options. You will need to enable that option manually via adb. To do so, connect your device to a computer with the Android SDK installed, and run the following command (one-line):\n\nadb shell settings put global force_desktop_mode_on_external_displays 1\n\n<b>What should I do if something goes wrong or my launcher / apps behave funky when desktop mode is enabled?</b>\n\nPlease follow these steps to disable desktop mode in case anything goes wrong:\n\n&#8226; Pull down the quick settings drawer, then enter system settings via the gear icon\n\n&#8226; Navigate to developer options and disable \"Enable freeform support\" and \"Force desktop mode\"\n\n&#8226; Reboot your device</string>

    <string name="tb_see_whats_new">See what\'s new in this release</string>
    <string name="tb_action_view">View</string>

    <string name="tb_error_creating_widget">Error creating widget</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast">Open desktop mode settings to enable a fix for visual glitches caused by input methods</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast_alt">Start desktop mode first before enabling the input method fix</string>

    <string name="tb_freeform_dialog_message">Um Apps mit Taskbar im Freiform-Fenstermodus zu öffnen, führen Sie bitte die folgenden Schritte aus:\n\n&#8226; Öffnen Sie die Entwickleroptionen\n\n&#8226; Scrollen Sie nach unten und aktivieren Sie »%s«\n\n&#8226; Starten Sie Ihr Gerät neu</string>

    <string name="tb_pref_title_invisible_button">Start-Schaltfläche einklappen, wenn Taskbar verborgen ist</string>
    <string name="tb_pref_title_freeform_mode_help">Hilfe und Anleitungen zum Freiform-Modus</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_title">Hilfe und Anleitungen</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_message"><b>Über den Freiform-Modus</b>\n\nMit Taskbar können Sie ab Android 7.0 Apps in schwebenden Fenstern öffnen. Root-Zugriff ist nicht erforderlich, obwohl Geräte mit Android 8.0, 8.1 und 9 einen adb-Shell-Befehl erfordern, der während der Ersteinrichtung ausgeführt werden muss.\n\nFolgen Sie einfach den folgenden Anweisungen, um Apps im Freiform-Modus zu öffnen:\n\n<b>1.</b> Aktivieren Sie »Unterstützung von Freiform-Fenstern« in der Taskbar-App\n\n<b>2.</b> Folgen Sie den Anweisungen, die im Popup-Fenster erscheinen, um (einmalig) die passenden Einstellungen auf Ihrem Gerät zu aktivieren\n\n<b>3.</b> Öffnen Sie die Übersicht der kürzlich verwendeten Apps auf Ihrem Gerät und schließen alle\n\n<b>4.</b> Starten Sie Taskbar und wählen eine App, um sie in einem Freiform-Fenster zu öffnen\n\n<i>Für beste Ergebnisse, besonders unter Android 8.0 und 8.1, starten Sie Ihr Gerät neu, bevor Sie den Freiform-Modus verwenden.</i>\n\n<b>Voreinstellungen für die Fenstergröße</b>\n\nTaskbar kann App-Fenster in verschiedenen voreingestellten Größen öffnen. Sie können eine Fenstergröße auswählen, indem Sie lange auf ein App-Icon drücken oder mit der rechten Maustaste darauf klicken und »Neues Fenster« wählen.\n\nUm eine Voreinstellung der Fenstergröße für eine bestimmte App zu speichern, drücken Sie einfach lange auf die gewünschte Größe, woraufhin ein Häkchen erscheint, das anzeigt, dass diese als Standard eingestellt ist. Taskbar startet die Anwendung beim nächsten Start mit der ausgewählten Größe.\n\nBeachten Sie, dass die Fenstergrößen unter Android 8.0 und 8.1 nicht korrekt funktionieren. Sie können dies jedoch umgehen, indem Sie die App öffnen, sie sofort wieder schließen und sie dann über das Übersichtsmenü von Android erneut öffnen.\n\n<b>Fehlerbehebung</b>\n\n<i>&#8226; Apps starten immer noch im Vollbildmodus</i>\n\nWenn eine Anwendung geöffnet wird, die bereits in den Speicher geladen ist, wird sie von Taskbar in den Vordergrund geholt. Um die App im Freiform-Modus zu öffnen, muss sie neu geöffnet werden.\n\nZiehen Sie die App aus der Übersicht weg und öffnen sie dann erneut über Taskbars letzte Apps. Wenn dies nicht funktioniert, drücken Sie lange auf das App-Icon, wählen »Infos zur App« und erzwingen dann das Beenden der App. Alternativ dazu können Sie Ihr Gerät neu starten.\n\n<i>&#8226; Maximierte Anwendungen können nicht in einem Fenster wiederhergestellt werden</i>\n\nWenn Sie die Maximieren-Schaltfläche in der Titelleiste einer App drücken, bringt Android die App aus dem Freiform-Modus in den Vollbild-Modus. Wie bereits erwähnt, kann Taskbar Apps nur dann in Freiform-Fenstern öffnen, wenn sie neu geöffnet werden.\n\nUm dieses Problem zu beheben, können Sie Fenster maximieren, indem Sie außerhalb des Fensterrahmens der App tippen oder klicken. Dadurch wird das Fenster so vergrößert, dass es den gesamten verfügbaren Platz ausfüllt, während es im Arbeitsbereich von Freiform-Fenstern bleibt.\n\n<i>&#8226; Das Maximieren von Fenstern funktioniert unter Android 9 nicht</i>\n\nDiese Funktionalität liegt außerhalb der Kontrolle der Taskbar und ist etwas, das Google seinerseits beheben muss. Wenn Sie ein Fenster unter Android 9 maximieren müssen, verwenden Sie stattdessen die Fenstergröße »Vollbild«.\n\n<i>&#8226; App-Übergänge scheinen besonders unter Android 9 nicht richtig zu funktionieren</i>\n\nDies ist ebenfalls ein Problem, das von Google behoben werden muss. Sie können es jedoch umgehen, indem Sie Übergangsanimationen deaktivieren.\n\nSie können Übergänge beim Öffnen einer App deaktivieren, indem Sie in den Taskbar-Einstellungen unter »Letzte Apps« die Option »Schnellerer App-Wechsel« aktivieren. Alternativ können Sie Übergänge systemweit deaktivieren, indem Sie in den Entwickleroptionen »Maßstab Übergangsanimation« auf »Animation aus« setzen.</string>

    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_message_1">Taskbar kann optional auch erweiterte Berechtigungen verwenden, um ihre Desktop-Erfahrung zu verbessern. Diese Berechtigungen werden durch einen adb-Shell-Befehl gewährt.\n\nWenn Sie fortfahren möchten, verbinden Sie ihr Gerät mit einem Computer, auf dem das Android-SDK installiert ist, und führen Sie diesen (einzeiligen) Befehl aus:</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_message_2">Einmal gewährt, werden die zusätzlichen Einstellungen aktiviert, wenn ein Display angeschlossen ist.</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_pref_description">Taskbar verwenden, um Apps auf einem externen Bildschirm in Fenstern zu öffnen, ähnlich wie beim Samsung DeX und andere Lösungen.\n\nBenötigt entweder einen USB-zu-HDMI-Adapter oder ein Lapdock und ein Gerät, das Video-Ausgabe unterstützt.</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_pref_description">Klicken Sie hier für Informationen zur Aktivierung</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_help"><b>Über den Desktop-Modus</b>\n\nTaskbar unterstützt die eingebaute Desktop-Modus-Funktionalität ab Android 10. Sie können ein kompatibles Gerät ab Android 10 mit einem externen Bildschirm verbinden und Apps in Fenstern öffnen, während Taskbars Benutzeroberfläche auf dem externen Bildschirm läuft, und ihr Launcher weiterhin auf ihrem Smartphone.\n\nDer Desktop-Modus benötigt einen USB-zu-HDMI-Adapter oder ein Lapdock und ein Gerät, das Video-Ausgabe unterstützt. Zusätzlich benötigen einige Einstellungen spezielle über adb gewährte Berechtigungen.\n\nUm anzufangen, aktivieren sie die Checkbox für »Desktop-Modus«. Die App wird sie durch den einmaligen Einrichtungsvorgang führen, der folgendes beinhaltet:\n\n&#8226; Aktivierung der Entwickleroptionen »Freiform-Fenster zulassen« und »Desktop-Modus erzwingen«\n\n&#8226; Festlegung von Taskbar als Startbildschirm\n\n&#8226; Neustart Ihres Geräts\n\n<b>Wieso muss Taskbar mein Startbildschirm sein, um den Desktop-Modus zu verwenden?</b>\n\nAndroid erlaubt es nur dem Standard-Launcher, auch als Launcher auf dem externen Display verwendet zu werden. Wenn die aktuell festgelegte Startbildschirm-App nicht explizit den Desktop-Modus unterstützt, verwendet Android einen eigenen Desktop-Modus.\n\nTaskbar kann dies umgehen, indem die App als Verbindung zwischen dem System und Ihrem bevorzugten Launcher arbeitet. Wenn Taskbar als Standard-Launcher festgelegt ist, wird es direkt Ihren bevorzugten Launcher starten und sich dann selbst beenden. Dieser Launcher wird in Taskbars Desktop-Modus-Einstellungen mit der Option »Standard-Launcher« festgelegt.\n\nEs gibt einige Dinge zu beachten, wenn Sie Taskbar als Standard-Launcher verwenden:\n\n&#8226; Die meisten App-Verknüpfungen (wenn sie lange auf das App-Icon drücken) werden nicht mehr angezeigt. Dies liegt daran, dass Android nur dem Standard-Launcher erlaubt, auf dynamisch erzeugte App-Verknüpfungen zuzugreifen.\n\n&#8226; Einige Animationen, die erfolgen, wenn die Startbildschirm-Schaltfläche gedrückt wird, werden eventuell fehlerhaft dargestellt. Dies ist auf das erwähnte Verhalten zurückzuführen, bei dem die Taskbar als Verbindung dient, wenn die Startbildschirm-Schaltfläche gedrückt wird.\n\n<b>Woher weiß ich, ob mein Gerät Video-Ausgabe auf externen Displays unterstützt?</b>\n\nNexDock betreibt eine Liste von Smartphones ab Android 10, die USB-zu-HDMI-Videoausgabe unterstüzen (diese Liste schließt Samsung- und Huawai-Geräte aus). Diese Liste ist hier verfügbar: http://nexdock.com/lapdock-for-smartphones\n\nInsbesondere Pixel-Geräte von Google unterstüzen Video-Ausgabe nicht. Trotzdem kann Taskbar auf diesen Geräten weiterhin über übliches Screen-Mirroring verwendet werden, entweder über Chromecast oder einem DisplayLink-Dock mit der zugehörigen Presenter-App.\n\n<b>Sollte ich Taskbars Desktop-Modus statt dem eingebauten Modus meines Gerätes verwenden, wie zum Beispiel Samsung DeX?</b>\n\nEinige Hersteller, wie Samsung und Huawei, haben ihren eigenen Desktop-Modus auf Basis des von Google in Android 10 eingebauten Desktop-Modus entwickelt. Diese Lösungen haben eine bessere Systemintegration und sind generell ausgefeilter. Es ist nicht nötig, zu Taskbar zu wechseln, wenn Sie mit dem Desktop-Modus ihres Geräts zufrieden sind.\n\nTaskbars Desktop-Modus ist für Geräte gedacht, die außer der von Google in Android 10 eingebauten Lösung keinen eigenen Desktop-Modus haben.\n\n<b>Ich finde die Option »Desktop-Modus erzwingen« in den Entwickleroptionen nicht &#8211; kann ich den Desktop-Modus trotzdem verwenden?</b>\n\nEinige Geräte (wie das ASUS ROG Phone 2) haben die Einstellung »Desktop-Modus erzwingen« in den Entwickleroptionen nicht. In diesem Fall müssen Sie die Option manuell über adb aktivieren. Verbinden Sie ihr Gerät dazu mit einem Computer, auf dem das Android-SDK installiert ist, und führen sie folgenden (einzeiligen) Befehl aus:\n\nadb shell settings put global force_desktop_mode_on_external_displays 1\n\n<b>Was soll ich tun, falls etwas schiefgeht oder sich mein Launcher bzw. meine Apps komisch verhalten, wenn der Desktop-Modus aktiv ist?</b>\n\nBitte folgen Sie diesen Schritten, um den Desktop-Modus zu deaktivieren, falls etwas schiefgeht:\n\n&#8226; Ziehen Sie das Schnelleinstellungsmenü herunter, dann öffnen Sie die Einstellungen mit dem Zahnradsymbol\n\n&#8226; Öffen Sie die Entwickleroptionen und deaktivieren sie dort »Freiform-Fenster zulassen« und »Desktop-Modus erzwingen«\n\n&#8226; Starten Sie ihr Gerät neu</string>

    <string name="tb_see_whats_new">Was ist neu in dieser Version?</string>
    <string name="tb_action_view">Anzeigen</string>

    <string name="tb_error_creating_widget">Fehler beim Erstellen des Widgets</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix">Fix für die Eingabemethode im Desktop-Modus</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_title">Fix für die Eingabemethode im Desktop-Modus aktivieren</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_summary">Behebt Anzeigefehler, die durch Eingabemethoden ab Android 11 verursacht werden. Sie müssen dazu bei jedem Start des Desktop-Modus vorübergehend Taskbar als Eingabemethode festlegen.</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast">Öffnen Sie die Einstellungen für den Desktop-Modus, um einen Fix für Anzeigefehler zu aktivieren, die durch Eingabemethoden verursacht werden</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast_alt">Starten Sie zuerst den Desktop-Modus, bevor Sie den Eingabemethode-Fix aktivieren</string>

    <string name="tb_rom">ROM</string>

    <string name="tb_override_freeform_unsupported">Erzwungene Aktivierung der Freiform-Modus-Einstellung</string>
    <string name="tb_override_freeform_unsupported_description">Diese Version von Android unterstützt den Freiform-Modus auf Displays nicht, deren Standard-Fenstermodus nicht auf Freiform eingestellt ist (wie z. B. das integrierte Gerätedisplay).\n\nWenn Sie glauben, dass dies ein Fehler ist, aktivieren Sie diese Einstellung, um die »Freiform-Modus«-Einstellung zu erzwingen.</string>

    <string name="tb_freeform_dialog_title">Activar soporte de ventanas libre</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_dialog_message">Para que la Barra de tareas pueda lanzar aplicaciones en modo ventana libre, por favor sigue estos pasos:\n\n&#8226; Ir a las Opciones de desarrollador\n\n&#8226; Tire hacia abajo y activa \"%s\"\n\n&#8226; Reinicie su dispositivo</string>

    <string name="tb_pref_title_invisible_button">Ocultar botón cuando la Barra de tareas se colapsa</string>
    <string name="tb_pref_title_freeform_mode_help">Ayuda e instrucciones para modo ventana libre</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_title">Ayuda e instrucciones</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_message"><b>Acerca del modo de ventanas libres</b>\n\nLa Barra de tareas te permite lanzar aplicaciones en ventanas flotantes de forma libre en dispositivos Android 7.0+.  No se requiere acceso de root, aunque los dispositivos Android 8.0, 8.1 y 9 requieren que se ejecute un comando adb shell durante la configuración inicial.\n\nSimplemente siga estos pasos para configurar su dispositivo para lanzar aplicaciones en modo ventana libre:\n\n<b>1.</b> Marque la casilla \"Soporte de ventana libre\" dentro de la aplicación de la barra de tareas.\n\n<b>2.</b> Siga las instrucciones que aparecen en la ventana emergente para activar la opción adecuada en su dispositivo (configuración única)\n\n<b>3.</b> Ve a la página de aplicaciones recientes de su dispositivo y borra todas las aplicaciones recientes\n\n<b>4.</b> Inicie la Barra de tareas, luego seleccione una aplicación para lanzarla en una ventana libre\n\n<i>Para obtener los mejores resultados, especialmente en Android 8.0 y 8.1, reinicie su dispositivo antes de usar el modo ventana libre</i>\n\n<b>Preajustes del tamaño de la ventana</b>\n\nLa barra de tareas puede lanzar ventanas de aplicaciones en varios tamaños preestablecidos.  Puede elegir el tamaño de la ventana presionando largamente o haciendo clic con el botón derecho del ratón en el icono de la aplicación y seleccionando \"Nueva ventana\".\n\nPara guardar un preajuste del tamaño de la ventana para una aplicación específica, sólo tiene que pulsar durante mucho tiempo el preajuste, y aparecerá una marca de verificación que indica que está configurado como predeterminado.  La barra de tareas lanzará la aplicación usando este tamaño seleccionado en lanzamientos posteriores.\n\nTenga en cuenta que los tamaños de las ventanas no funcionan correctamente en Android 8.0 y 8.1.  Sin embargo, puede solucionarlo iniciando la aplicación, cerrándola inmediatamente y volviéndola a iniciar desde el menú general de Android.\n\n<b>Problemas conocidos</b>\n\n<i>&#8226; Las aplicaciones aún se lanzan a pantalla completa</i>\n\nSi se lanza una aplicación que ya está cargada en la memoria, la barra de tareas la pondrá en primer plano. Para lanzar la aplicación en modo ventana libre, tiene que lanzarse en un nuevo estado.\n\nBorra la aplicación de los recientes, y luego vuelve a lanzarla desde la página de aplicaciones recientes usando la barra de tareas. Si esto no funciona, entonces presiona el icono de la aplicación, selecciona "Información de la aplicación" y luego fuerce la detención de la aplicación.  Alternativamente, puede reiniciar su dispositivo.\n\n<i>&#8226; Las aplicaciones que se maximizan no pueden ser restauradas en una ventana</i>\n\nSi pulsas el botón Maximizar en la barra de título de una aplicación, Android sacará la aplicación del modo ventana libre y la pondrá en modo de pantalla completa. Como se mencionó anteriormente, la barra de tareas sólo puede lanzar aplicaciones en ventanas de forma libre si se lanzan desde un nuevo estado.\n\nPara solucionarlo, puedes maximizar las ventanas tocando o haciendo clic en el marco exterior de la ventana de la aplicación.  Esto expandirá la ventana para llenar todo el espacio disponible mientras la mantiene en el espacio de trabajo de la ventana de forma libre.\n\n<i>&#8226; Maximizar las ventanas no funciona en Android 9</i>\n\nEsta funcionalidad no es controlada por la Barra de tareas y es algo que Google necesita arreglar a profundidad. Si necesitas maximizar una ventana en Android 9, por favor usa el tamaño de ventana "Maximizado" en su lugar.\n\n<i>&#8226; Las transiciones de las aplicaciones no parecen funcionar correctamente, especialmente en Android 9</i>\n\nEsto también es un problema que Google necesita arreglar a profundidad, sin embargo, puede solucionarlo deshabilitando las transiciones de las aplicaciones\n\nPuede deshabilitar las transiciones al lanzar una aplicación yendo a los ajustes de aplicaciones recientes de la Barra de tareas y habilitando la opción \"Cambio rápido de aplicaciones\". Alternativamente, puede deshabilitar las transiciones en todo el sistema yendo a las opciones del desarrollador y configurando \"Escala de animación de transición\" a \"Animación desactivada\".</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_pref_description">Utilizar la barra de tareas para lanzar aplicaciones en ventana en una pantalla externa, similar a Samsung DeX y otras soluciones.\n\nRequiere un adaptador de USB a HDMI, o un lapdock, y un dispositivo que soporte la salida de video.</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_pref_description">Clic aquí para la información sobre cómo activar</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_help"><b>Acerca del modo de escritorio</b>\n\nTaskbar es compatible con el modo de escritorio incorporado de Android 10. Puede conectar su dispositivocompatible con Android 10+ a una pantalla externa y ejecutar aplicaciones en ventanas redimensionables, con la interfaz de Taskbar ejecutándose en su pantalla externa y su lanzador existente todavía ejecutándose en su teléfono. El modo de escritorio requiere un adaptador de USB a HDMI (o un lapdock), y un dispositivo compatible que soporte la salida de video. Además, ciertos ajustes requieren la concesión de un permiso especial a través de adb.\n\nPara empezar, marque la casilla de \"Modo escritorio\". La aplicación le guiará a través del proceso de configuración de una sola vez, que implica:\n\n&#8226; Habilitar las opciones de desarrollo \"Habilitar modo ventana libre\" y \"Forzar el modo de escritorio\"\n\n&#8226; Establecer Taskbar como aplicación de inicio predeterminada\n\n&#8226; Reiniciar el dispositivo\n\n<b>¿Por qué es necesario establecer Taskbar como mi aplicación de inicio predeterminada para que funcione el modo de escritorio?</b>\n\nPor diseño, Android sólo permitirá que la aplicación de inicio predeterminada actúe como lanzador del modo de escritorio en las pantallas externas.  Si la aplicación predeterminada no declara específicamente el soporte para el modo de escritorio, Android volverá a usar una interfaz de modo de escritorio predefinida.\n\nTaskbar es capaz de trabajar alrededor de esta limitación actuando como un calce entre el sistema y su lanzador preferido.  Cuando la Barra de tareas se configura como su aplicación de inicio, al presionar el botón de inicio se iniciará inmediatamente el lanzador que prefieras usar en su teléfono, y luego saldrá de ella.  Este lanzador se configura en las preferencias del modo de escritorio de la Taskbar haciendo clic en la opción \"Lanzador principal\".\n\nHay un par de advertencias a tener en cuenta cuando Taskbar se establece como su aplicación de inicio:\n\n&#8226; La mayoría de accesos directos a las aplicaciones (que se ven al pulsar el icono de una aplicación) ya no estarán disponibles para su lanzador principal.  Esto se debe a una limitación del sistema Android, en el que sólo la aplicación de inicio predeterminada tiene acceso a los accesos directos de la aplicación que se crean dinámicamente.\n\n&#8226; Ciertas animaciones que ocurren al presionar el botón de inicio podrían lucirse inexactas.  Esto se debe al comportamiento mencionado anteriormente, en el que la Barra de tareas actúa como un calce cuando se presiona el botón de inicio.\n\n<b>¿Cómo sé si mi dispositivo admite la salida de vídeo a pantallas externas?</b>\n\nLos desarrolladores de NexDock mantienen una lista de teléfonos inteligentes compatibles con Android 10+ junto a la salida de video USB a HDMI (esta lista excluye los dispositivos Samsung y Huawei).  Puedes ver esta lista aquí: http://nexdock.com/lapdock-for-smartphones\n\nLos dispositivos de Google Pixel, en particular, no incluyen soporte para salida de video.  Sin embargo, puedes utilizar Taskbar con la duplicación de pantalla estándar en estos dispositivos, ya sea a través de Chromecast o mediante el uso de un acoplamiento DisplayLink junto con la aplicación DisplayLink Presenter.\n\n<b>¿Debo usar el modo escritorio de Taskbar sobre la solución de modo de escritorio incorporada en mi dispositivo, como el Samsung DeX?</b>\n\nAlgunos OEMs, como Samsung y Huawei, han implementado su propia versión del modo de escritorio, además de la implementación de Google en Android 10.  Estas soluciones tienen una mejor integración con el resto del sistema y son generalmente más pulidas en general.  No es necesario cambiar a Taskbar si está contento con la solución de modo de escritorio existente en su dispositivo.\n\nLa compatibilidad con el modo de escritorio de Taskbar está pensada hacia los dispositivos que no ofrecen una solución de modo de escritorio incorporada más allá de lo que Google ofrece en Android 10.\n\n<b>No encuentro la opción \"Forzar el modo de escritorio\" en las opciones de desarrollo &#8211; ¿puedo seguir usando el modo de escritorio?</b>\n\nAlgunos dispositivos (como el teléfono ASUS ROG 2) no tienen la opción de \"Forzar el modo de escritorio\" en las opciones de desarrollo. Necesitará habilitar esa opción manualmente a través de adb. Para ello, conecte su dispositivo a un ordenador con el SDK de Android instalado y ejecuta el siguiente comando (una línea):\n\nadb shell settings put global force_desktop_mode_on_external_displays 1\n\n<b>¿Qué debo hacer si algo sale mal o si mi lanzador o las aplicaciones se comportan de manera extraña cuando el modo de escritorio está activado?</b>\n\nPor favor, siga estos pasos para desactivar el modo de escritorio en caso de que algo vaya mal:\n\n&#8226; Tire hacia abajo del cajón de ajustes rápidos, y luego introduzca los ajustes del sistema a través del icono del engranaje\n\n&#8226; Navega a las opciones del desarrollador y desactive \"Activar soporte de ventana libre\" y \"Forzar el modo de escritorio\"\n\n&#8226; Reinicie su dispositivo</string>

    <string name="tb_see_whats_new">Ver que hay de nuevo en este lanzamiento</string>
    <string name="tb_action_view">Ver</string>

    <string name="tb_error_creating_widget">Error creating widget</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix">Desktop mode input method fix</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_title">Enable input method fix</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_summary">Fixes visual glitches caused by input methods on Android 11+. Requires you to temporarily set Taskbar as your input method each time desktop mode starts.</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast">Open desktop mode settings to enable a fix for visual glitches caused by input methods</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast_alt">Start desktop mode first before enabling the input method fix</string>

    <string name="tb_rom">ROM</string>

    <string name="tb_override_freeform_unsupported">Force-enable freeform mode settings</string>

    <string name="tb_freeform_dialog_message">Taskbar がフリーフォーム ウインドウ モードでアプリを起動するために、次の手順に従ってください:\n\n&#8226; 開発者オプションに移動します\n\n&#8226; 最下部にスクロールして \"%s\" をオンにします\n\n&#8226; お使いのデバイスを再起動します</string>

    <string name="tb_pref_title_invisible_button">Taskbar を折りたたんでいるときボタンを非表示</string>
    <string name="tb_pref_title_freeform_mode_help">ヘルプ &amp; フリーフォームモードの説明</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_title">ヘルプ &amp; 説明</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_message"><b>フリーフォームモードについて</b>\n\nTaskbar は Android 7.0+ (Nougat) 上でアプリをフリーフォーム フローティング ウインドウで起動することができます。ルート アクセス許可は必要がありません。フリーフォームモードでアプリを起動するには次の手順に従ってください:\n\n<b>1.</b> Taskbar アプリ内で \"フリーフォーム ウインドウ サポート\" のボックスをチェックします\n\n<b>2.</b> ポップアップで表示される向きに従って、お使いのデバイスに適切な設定を有効にします\n\n<b>3.</b> Taskbar をオンにして、お使いのデバイスの最近使用したアプリのページに移動します\n\n<b>4.</b> 最近使用したアプリをすべてクリアして、(空の) 最近使用したアプリのページに戻ります\n\n<b>5.</b> Taskbar の開始ボタンを押して、フリーフォーム ウインドウで起動するアプリを選択します\n\n<b>トラブルシューティング</b>\n\n<i>&#8226; アプリがまだ全画面モードで起動します</i>\n\n起動するアプリがすでにメモリにロードされている場合、Taskbar はそれをフォアグラウンドに移動します。フリーフォームモードでアプリを起動するため、新規の状態から起動する必要があります。\n\n最近使用したアプリからアプリをアプリを外にスワイプして、Taskbar を使用して最近使用したアプリのページから再起移動します。これが動作しない場合は、アプリのアイコンを長押しして、\"アプリ情報\" を選択し、アプリを強制的に停止します。\n\nTaskbar は、お使いのデバイスの最近使用したアプリから、ホーム画面から、フリーフォームワークスペース自身からのみアプリをフリーフォームモードで起動できることにご注意ください。別の全画面アプリからアプリを起動すると、同じく全画面モードで起動されます。\n\n<i>&#8226; 最大化されたアプリがウインドウに復元できません</i>\n\nアプリのタイトルバーで最大化ボタンを押した場合、Android はアプリをフリーフォームモードの外に出して、全画面モードに移動します。そのため、新規の状態から起動した場合のみ Taskbar はアプリをフリーフォームウインドウで起動することができます。\n\nこれを修正するために、アプリのウインドウ フレームの外側をクリックしてウインドウを最大化することができます。これによりフリーフォーム ウインドウ ワークスペースに残ったまま、利用可能なスペースすべてにウインドウを広げます。\n\n<b>完全にシステムレベルのフリーフォーム ウインドウ サポートを有効にする</b>\n\nAndroid SDK をインストールしたコンピュータが利用できる場合、次の adb シェルコマンドを実行して、システムレベルでフリーフォームモードのサポートを有効にすることができます:\n\nsettings put global enable_freeform_support 1\n\n上記のコマンドを実行した後、お使いのデバイスを再起動します。お使いのデバイスの最近使用したアプリのページで、アプリのエントリーに、指定したアプリをフリーフォームモードに入る/終了する新しいボタンが表示されます。</string>

    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_message_2">Once granted, the additional settings will be enabled when a display is connected.</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_pref_description">Use Taskbar to launch windowed apps on an external display, similar to Samsung DeX and other solutions.\n\nRequires either a USB-to-HDMI adapter, or a lapdock, and a device that supports video output.</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_pref_description">Click here for info on how to enable</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_help"><b>About desktop mode</b>\n\nTaskbar supports Android 10\'s built-in desktop mode functionality. You can connect your compatible Android 10+ device to an external display and run apps in resizable windows, with Taskbar\'s interface running on your external display and your existing launcher still running on your phone.\n\nDesktop mode requires a USB-to-HDMI adapter (or a lapdock), and a compatible device that supports video output. Additionally, certain settings require granting a special permission via adb.\n\nTo get started, tick the checkbox for \"Desktop mode.\" The app will guide you through the one-time setup process, which involves:\n\n&#8226; Enabling the \"Enable freeform windows\" and \"Force desktop mode\" developer options\n\n&#8226; Setting Taskbar as your default home app\n\n&#8226; Rebooting your device\n\n<b>Why does Taskbar need to be set as my default home app for desktop mode to work?</b>\n\nBy design, Android will only allow the default home app to act as the launcher for desktop mode on external displays.  If the currently set home app does not specifically declare support for desktop mode, then Android will fall back to using a predefined desktop mode interface.\n\nTaskbar is able to work around this limitation by acting as a shim between the system and your preferred launcher.  When Taskbar is set as your home app, when the home button is pressed it will immediately start the launcher you prefer to use on your phone, then quit.  This launcher is set within Taskbar\'s desktop mode preferences by clicking on the \"Primary launcher\" setting.\n\nThere are a couple of caveats to keep in mind when Taskbar is set as your home app:\n\n&#8226; Most app shortcuts (seen when long-pressing an app icon) will no longer be available to your primary launcher.  This is due to an Android system limitation where only the default home app has access to app shortcuts that are dynamically created.\n\n&#8226; Certain animations that occur when pressing the home button may look incorrect.  This is due to the aforementioned behavior where Taskbar acts as a shim when the home button is pressed.\n\n<b>How do I know if my device supports video output to external displays?</b>\n\nThe folks over at NexDock maintain a list of Android 10+ smartphones that are compatible with USB-to-HDMI video output (this list excludes Samsung and Huawei devices).  You can view this list here: http://nexdock.com/lapdock-for-smartphones\n\nGoogle Pixel devices, notably, do not include support for video output.  However, you can still use Taskbar with standard screen mirroring on these devices, either via Chromecast or by using a DisplayLink dock coupled with the DisplayLink Presenter app.\n\n<b>Should I use Taskbar\'s desktop mode over my device\'s built-in desktop mode solution, such as Samsung DeX?</b>\n\nSome OEMs, such as Samsung and Huawei, have implemented their own version of desktop mode, building on top of Google\'s implementation in Android 10.  These solutions have better integration with the rest of the system and are generally more polished overall.  There is no need to switch over to Taskbar if you\'re happy with your device\'s existing desktop mode solution.\n\nTaskbar\'s desktop mode support is geared towards devices that don\'t offer a built-in desktop mode solution beyond what Google ships as stock with Android 10.\n\n<b>I can\'t find the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options &#8211; can I still use desktop mode?</b>\n\nSome devices (such as the ASUS ROG Phone 2) do not have the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options. You will need to enable that option manually via adb. To do so, connect your device to a computer with the Android SDK installed, and run the following command (one-line):\n\nadb shell settings put global force_desktop_mode_on_external_displays 1\n\n<b>What should I do if something goes wrong or my launcher / apps behave funky when desktop mode is enabled?</b>\n\nPlease follow these steps to disable desktop mode in case anything goes wrong:\n\n&#8226; Pull down the quick settings drawer, then enter system settings via the gear icon\n\n&#8226; Navigate to developer options and disable \"Enable freeform support\" and \"Force desktop mode\"\n\n&#8226; Reboot your device</string>

    <string name="tb_see_whats_new">See what\'s new in this release</string>
    <string name="tb_action_view">View</string>

    <string name="tb_error_creating_widget">Error creating widget</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast_alt">Start desktop mode first before enabling the input method fix</string>

    <string name="tb_pref_title_invisible_button">Knop verbergen als Taakbalk is ingeklapt</string>
    <string name="tb_pref_title_freeform_mode_help">Hulp en instructies over vrije venstermodus</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_title">Hulp en instructies</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_message"><b>Over vrije venstermodus</b>\n\nTaakbalk stelt je in staat om apps op te starten in vrije, verplaatsbare vensters op apparaten met Android 7.0 en hoger; hiervoor is roottoegang vereist.  Op apparaten met Android 8.0 en hoger is alleen een adb shell-opdracht vereist.\n\nVolg deze stappen om je apparaat in te stellen zodat apps kunnen worden opgestart in vrije venstermodus:\n\n<b>1.</b> Schakel \"Vrije venstermodus-ondersteuning\" in in de Taakbalk-app\n\n<b>2.</b> Volg de instructies in het pop-upvenster om de juiste instellingen te activeren op je apparaat (eenmalig)\n\n<b>3.</b> Ga naar het recente apps-overzicht en sluit alle recente apps\n\n<b>4.</b> Start Taakbalk en kies een app om deze op te starten in vrije venstermodus\n\n<i>Voor het beste resultaat, vooral op Android 8.0 en hoger, herstart je je apparaat vóórdat je vrije venstermodus gebruikt</i>\n\n<b>Voorinstellingen voor vensterafmetingen</b>\n\nTaakbalk kan app-vensters openen met een voorinstelling.  Kies een vensterafmeting door een app-pictogram lang ingedrukt te houden of door er met de rechtermuisknop op te klikken en kies \"Nieuw venster.\"\n\nOm een voorinstelling op te slaan voor een specifieke app, houd je de voorinstelling lang ingedrukt. Er verschijnt dan een vinkje, wat aangeeft dat het de standaardinstelling is geworden.  Taakbalk start de app voortaan op met de gekozen afmetingen.\n\nLet op: dit werkt niet altijd juist op Android 8.0 en 8.1.  Je kunt dit verbeteren door de app op te starten, daarna direct te minimaliseren en weer te openen via het recente apps-overzicht.\n\n<b>Probleemoplossing</b>\n\n<i>&#8226; Apps starten nog steeds op in volledig scherm-modus</i>\n\nAls een app wordt opgestart die al in het geheugen is geladen, dan brengt Taakbalk hem naar de voorgrond. Om de app op te starten in vrije venstermodus, moet de app een koude start krijgen.\n\nVeeg de app weg uit het recente apps-overzicht en start de app opnieuw. Als dit niet werkt, houd dan het app-pictogram lang ingedrukt, kies \"App-informatie\" en sluit de app geforceerd af.  Ook kun je je apparaat herstarten.\n\n<i>&#8226; Gemaximaliseerde apps kunnen niet worden verkleind naar een venster</i>\n\nAls je op de maximaliseer-knop drukt, dan haalt Android de app uit de vrije venstermodus en toont hem op het volledige scherm. Zoals hierboven beschreven, kan Taakbalk alleen apps starten in vrije venstermodus als de apps koud worden gestart.\n\nOm dit te omzeilen, kun je vensters maximaliseren door net buiten het vensterframe te drukken of klikken.  Dit zorgt ervoor dat de app alle beschikbare ruimte opvult zonder dat deze uit de vrije venstermodus wordt gehaald.\n\n<i>&#8226; Vensters maximaliseren werkt niet op Android 9</i>\n\nDit ligt buiten de macht van Taakbalk en is iets dat Google moet oplossen. Gebruik bovenstaande methode om een venster te maximaliseren op Android 9.\n\n<i>&#8226; App-overgangen werken niet goed, vooral op Android 9</i>\n\nOok dit is iets dat Google moet oplossen. Echter, je kunt dit omzeilen door app-overgangen uit te schakelen.\n\nDit kan door naar de recente app-instellingen in de Taakbalk-instellingen te gaan en de optie \"Sneller schakelen tussen apps\" in te schakelen. Je kunt dit ook systeembreed doen door in de instellingen te gaan naar Ontwikkelaarsopties en bij \"Vensterovergangsschaal\" te kiezen voor \"Geen animaties.\"</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_dialog_message">To configure your device for desktop mode, please follow these steps:\n\n&#8226; Go to Developer Options\n\n&#8226; Scroll to the bottom and turn on \"Enable freeform windows\" and \"Force desktop mode\"\n\n&#8226; Set Taskbar as your default home app\n\n&#8226; Reboot your device\n\nFor more information and troubleshooting steps, click the (?) icon in the upper-right hand corner of the screen.</string>

    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_message_2">Once granted, the additional settings will be enabled when a display is connected.</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_pref_description">Use Taskbar to launch windowed apps on an external display, similar to Samsung DeX and other solutions.\n\nRequires either a USB-to-HDMI adapter, or a lapdock, and a device that supports video output.</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_pref_description">Click here for info on how to enable</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_help"><b>About desktop mode</b>\n\nTaskbar supports Android 10\'s built-in desktop mode functionality. You can connect your compatible Android 10+ device to an external display and run apps in resizable windows, with Taskbar\'s interface running on your external display and your existing launcher still running on your phone.\n\nDesktop mode requires a USB-to-HDMI adapter (or a lapdock), and a compatible device that supports video output. Additionally, certain settings require granting a special permission via adb.\n\nTo get started, tick the checkbox for \"Desktop mode.\" The app will guide you through the one-time setup process, which involves:\n\n&#8226; Enabling the \"Enable freeform windows\" and \"Force desktop mode\" developer options\n\n&#8226; Setting Taskbar as your default home app\n\n&#8226; Rebooting your device\n\n<b>Why does Taskbar need to be set as my default home app for desktop mode to work?</b>\n\nBy design, Android will only allow the default home app to act as the launcher for desktop mode on external displays.  If the currently set home app does not specifically declare support for desktop mode, then Android will fall back to using a predefined desktop mode interface.\n\nTaskbar is able to work around this limitation by acting as a shim between the system and your preferred launcher.  When Taskbar is set as your home app, when the home button is pressed it will immediately start the launcher you prefer to use on your phone, then quit.  This launcher is set within Taskbar\'s desktop mode preferences by clicking on the \"Primary launcher\" setting.\n\nThere are a couple of caveats to keep in mind when Taskbar is set as your home app:\n\n&#8226; Most app shortcuts (seen when long-pressing an app icon) will no longer be available to your primary launcher.  This is due to an Android system limitation where only the default home app has access to app shortcuts that are dynamically created.\n\n&#8226; Certain animations that occur when pressing the home button may look incorrect.  This is due to the aforementioned behavior where Taskbar acts as a shim when the home button is pressed.\n\n<b>How do I know if my device supports video output to external displays?</b>\n\nThe folks over at NexDock maintain a list of Android 10+ smartphones that are compatible with USB-to-HDMI video output (this list excludes Samsung and Huawei devices).  You can view this list here: http://nexdock.com/lapdock-for-smartphones\n\nGoogle Pixel devices, notably, do not include support for video output.  However, you can still use Taskbar with standard screen mirroring on these devices, either via Chromecast or by using a DisplayLink dock coupled with the DisplayLink Presenter app.\n\n<b>Should I use Taskbar\'s desktop mode over my device\'s built-in desktop mode solution, such as Samsung DeX?</b>\n\nSome OEMs, such as Samsung and Huawei, have implemented their own version of desktop mode, building on top of Google\'s implementation in Android 10.  These solutions have better integration with the rest of the system and are generally more polished overall.  There is no need to switch over to Taskbar if you\'re happy with your device\'s existing desktop mode solution.\n\nTaskbar\'s desktop mode support is geared towards devices that don\'t offer a built-in desktop mode solution beyond what Google ships as stock with Android 10.\n\n<b>I can\'t find the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options &#8211; can I still use desktop mode?</b>\n\nSome devices (such as the ASUS ROG Phone 2) do not have the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options. You will need to enable that option manually via adb. To do so, connect your device to a computer with the Android SDK installed, and run the following command (one-line):\n\nadb shell settings put global force_desktop_mode_on_external_displays 1\n\n<b>What should I do if something goes wrong or my launcher / apps behave funky when desktop mode is enabled?</b>\n\nPlease follow these steps to disable desktop mode in case anything goes wrong:\n\n&#8226; Pull down the quick settings drawer, then enter system settings via the gear icon\n\n&#8226; Navigate to developer options and disable \"Enable freeform support\" and \"Force desktop mode\"\n\n&#8226; Reboot your device</string>

    <string name="tb_see_whats_new">See what\'s new in this release</string>
    <string name="tb_action_view">View</string>

    <string name="tb_error_creating_widget">Error creating widget</string>

- Przejdź do opcji dla programistów i włącz debugowanie USB.

adb shell settings put global enable_freeform_support 1

<string name="tb_freeform_dialog_title">Włącz obsługę Freeform</string>
<string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_message">"Tryb freeform (swobodne okna)

Pasek zadań umożliwia uruchamianie aplikacji w dowolnych ruchomych oknach na urządzeniach z Androidem 7.0 lub więcej. Nie jest wymagany dostęp do root, chociaż urządzenia z systemem Android 8,0, 8,1 i 9 wymagają polecenia powłoki ADB do uruchomienia podczas wstępnej konfiguracji.

Po prostu wykonaj następujące kroki, aby skonfigurować urządzenie do uruchamiania aplikacji w trybie Freeform:

1. Zaznacz pole dla \"Obsługa okien Freeform\" wewnątrz aplikacji Taskbar

2. Postępuj zgodnie ze wskazówkami wyświetlanymi w oknie podręcznym, aby włączyć odpowiednie ustawienia w urządzeniu (jednorazowa konfiguracja)

3. Przejdź do strony ostatnich aplikacji na urządzeniu i wyczyść wszystkie ostatnie aplikacje

4. Uruchom Taskbar, a następnie wybierz aplikację, aby ją uruchomić w oknie freeform (swobodne okno)

Aby uzyskać najlepsze wyniki, zwłaszcza w systemie Android 8,0 i 8,1, uruchom ponownie urządzenie przed użyciem trybu freeform

Ustawienia predefiniowane rozmiaru okna

Pasek zadań może uruchamiać okna aplikacji w różnych predefiniowanych rozmiarach. Możesz wybrać rozmiar okna przez długie naciśnięcie lub kliknięcie prawym przyciskiem myszy ikony aplikacji i wybranie \"nowe okno.\"

Aby zapisać predefiniowany rozmiar okna dla konkretnej aplikacji, po prostu naciśnij i przytrzymaj preset, a pojawi się znacznik wyboru wskazujący, że jest ustawiony jako domyślny. Pasek zadań uruchomi aplikację przy użyciu tego wybranego rozmiaru po kolejnym uruchomieniu.

Pamiętaj, że rozmiary okien nie działają poprawnie na Androidzie 8,0 i 8,1. Jednak można obejść to, uruchamiając aplikację, i natychmiast zamykając ją, a następnie uruchamiając ją ponownie z menu przeglądnia Androida.

Rozwiązywanie problemów

• Aplikacje nadal uruchomić na pełnym ekranie

Jeśli aplikacja jest uruchomiona, która jest już załadowana do pamięci, pasek zadań przyniesie ją na pierwszy plan. W celu uruchomienia aplikacji w trybie Freeform, musi być uruchomiona ze świeżego stanu.

Przesuń aplikację z dala od Ostatnie, a następnie ponownie uruchom ją ze strony ostatnich aplikacji za pomocą Taskbar. Jeśli to nie zadziała, naciśnij długo ikonę aplikacji, wybierz \"informacje o aplikacji,\", a następnie Wymuś zatrzymanie aplikacji. Alternatywnie można ponownie uruchomić urządzenie.

• Aplikacje, które są zmaksymalizowane, nie mogą zostać przywrócone do okna

Jeśli naciśniesz przycisk Maksymalizuj na pasku tytułowym aplikacji, Android przyniesie aplikację z trybu swobodnego i do trybu pełnoekranowego. Jak wspomniano powyżej, Taskbar może uruchamiać aplikacje tylko w oknach o swobodnym kształcie, jeśli są uruchamiane ze świeżego stanu.

Aby rozwiązać ten problem, można zmaksymalizować okna, naciskając lub klikając tuż poza ramkę okna aplikacji. Spowoduje to rozwinięcie okna, aby wypełnić wszystkie dostępne miejsca, zachowując je w obszarze roboczym freeform.

• Maksymalizacja systemu Windows nie działa na Androidzie 9

Ta funkcjonalność wykracza poza kontrolę Taskbar i jest czymś, co Google musi naprawić na ich końcu. Jeśli chcesz zmaksymalizować okno na Androidzie 9, użyj \"zmaksymalizowanego\" rozmiaru okna.

• Przejścia aplikacji nie wydają się działać prawidłowo, zwłaszcza na Androidzie 9

Jest to również problem, który Google musi naprawić na ich końcu, jednak można obejść, wyłączając przejścia aplikacji.

Możesz wyłączyć przejścia podczas uruchamiania aplikacji, przechodząc do ustawień ostatnich aplikacji na Taskbar i włączając opcję \"szybsze przełączanie aplikacji\". Alternatywnie można wyłączyć przejścia w całym systemie, przechodząc do opcji programisty i ustawiając \"Skala animacji przejścia\" na \"Animacja wyłączona.\""</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_dialog_message">To configure your device for desktop mode, please follow these steps:\n\n&#8226; Go to Developer Options\n\n&#8226; Scroll to the bottom and turn on \"Enable freeform windows\" and \"Force desktop mode\"\n\n&#8226; Set Taskbar as your default home app\n\n&#8226; Reboot your device\n\nFor more information and troubleshooting steps, click the (?) icon in the upper-right hand corner of the screen.</string>

These permissions are granted by running an adb shell command.\n\nIf you wish to continue, connect your device to a computer with the Android SDK installed, and run the following command (one line):</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_message_2">Once granted, the additional settings will be enabled when a display is connected.</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_pref_description">Use Taskbar to launch windowed apps on an external display, similar to Samsung DeX and other solutions.\n\nRequires either a USB-to-HDMI adapter, or a lapdock, and a device that supports video output.</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_pref_description">Click here for info on how to enable</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_help"><b>About desktop mode</b>\n\nTaskbar supports Android 10\'s built-in desktop mode functionality. You can connect your compatible Android 10+ device to an external display and run apps in resizable windows, with Taskbar\'s interface running on your external display and your existing launcher still running on your phone.\n\nDesktop mode requires a USB-to-HDMI adapter (or a lapdock), and a compatible device that supports video output. Additionally, certain settings require granting a special permission via adb.\n\nTo get started, tick the checkbox for \"Desktop mode.\" The app will guide you through the one-time setup process, which involves:\n\n&#8226; Enabling the \"Enable freeform windows\" and \"Force desktop mode\" developer options\n\n&#8226; Setting Taskbar as your default home app\n\n&#8226; Rebooting your device\n\n<b>Why does Taskbar need to be set as my default home app for desktop mode to work?</b>\n\nBy design, Android will only allow the default home app to act as the launcher for desktop mode on external displays.  If the currently set home app does not specifically declare support for desktop mode, then Android will fall back to using a predefined desktop mode interface.\n\nTaskbar is able to work around this limitation by acting as a shim between the system and your preferred launcher.  When Taskbar is set as your home app, when the home button is pressed it will immediately start the launcher you prefer to use on your phone, then quit.  This launcher is set within Taskbar\'s desktop mode preferences by clicking on the \"Primary launcher\" setting.\n\nThere are a couple of caveats to keep in mind when Taskbar is set as your home app:\n\n&#8226; Most app shortcuts (seen when long-pressing an app icon) will no longer be available to your primary launcher.  This is due to an Android system limitation where only the default home app has access to app shortcuts that are dynamically created.\n\n&#8226; Certain animations that occur when pressing the home button may look incorrect.  This is due to the aforementioned behavior where Taskbar acts as a shim when the home button is pressed.\n\n<b>How do I know if my device supports video output to external displays?</b>\n\nThe folks over at NexDock maintain a list of Android 10+ smartphones that are compatible with USB-to-HDMI video output (this list excludes Samsung and Huawei devices).  You can view this list here: http://nexdock.com/lapdock-for-smartphones\n\nGoogle Pixel devices, notably, do not include support for video output.  However, you can still use Taskbar with standard screen mirroring on these devices, either via Chromecast or by using a DisplayLink dock coupled with the DisplayLink Presenter app.\n\n<b>Should I use Taskbar\'s desktop mode over my device\'s built-in desktop mode solution, such as Samsung DeX?</b>\n\nSome OEMs, such as Samsung and Huawei, have implemented their own version of desktop mode, building on top of Google\'s implementation in Android 10.  These solutions have better integration with the rest of the system and are generally more polished overall.  There is no need to switch over to Taskbar if you\'re happy with your device\'s existing desktop mode solution.\n\nTaskbar\'s desktop mode support is geared towards devices that don\'t offer a built-in desktop mode solution beyond what Google ships as stock with Android 10.\n\n<b>I can\'t find the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options &#8211; can I still use desktop mode?</b>\n\nSome devices (such as the ASUS ROG Phone 2) do not have the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options. You will need to enable that option manually via adb. To do so, connect your device to a computer with the Android SDK installed, and run the following command (one-line):\n\nadb shell settings put global force_desktop_mode_on_external_displays 1\n\n<b>What should I do if something goes wrong or my launcher / apps behave funky when desktop mode is enabled?</b>\n\nPlease follow these steps to disable desktop mode in case anything goes wrong:\n\n&#8226; Pull down the quick settings drawer, then enter system settings via the gear icon\n\n&#8226; Navigate to developer options and disable \"Enable freeform support\" and \"Force desktop mode\"\n\n&#8226; Reboot your device</string>

    <string name="tb_see_whats_new">See what\'s new in this release</string>

    <string name="tb_freeform_dialog_message">Для запуска приложений в режиме Freeform:\n\n&#8226; Откройте Настройки Разработчика\n\n&#8226; Прокрутите вниз и включите \"%s\"\n\n&#8226; Перезагрузите устройство</string>

    <string name="tb_pref_title_invisible_button">Спрятать кнопку Taskbar при скрытии</string>
    <string name="tb_pref_title_freeform_mode_help">Инструкции для режима Freeform</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_title">Инструкции</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_message"><b>О режиме</b>\n\nTaskbar разрешает запуск приложений в режиме Freeform на Android 7.0+ без root-прав. Просто выполните следующие шаги:\n\n<b>1.</b> Проверьте настройку \"Включить Freeform\" внутри Taskbar.\n\n<b>2.</b> Следуйте указаниям в новом окне\n\n<b>3.</b> С включенным Taskbar, откройте список недавних приложений.\n\n<b>4.</b> Закройте все окна там и откройте список снова.\n\n<b>5.</b> Нажмите на меню Taskbar и нажмите на приложение, которое вы хотите запустить в режиме Freeform \n\n<b>Решение проблем</b>\n\n<i>&#8226; Приложения все равно запускаются в полноэкранном режиме</i>\n\nЕсли приложение уже запущено, Taskbar покажет его на переднем плане. Чтобы запустить приложение в режиме Freeform mode, оно должно запуститься заново.\n\n Если это не работает, остановите приложение.\n\nTaskbar может запускать программы в режиме Freeform только со списка недавних приложений, с домашнего экрана или с рабочего стола Freeform. Запуск приложения в полноэкранном приложении запустит его в полноэкранном режиме тоже.\n\n<i>&#8226; Развернутые приложения нельзя вернуть в нормальный размер</i>\n\nЕсли вы нажмете кнопку "Развернуть" на окне приложения, Android вернет приложение в полноэкранный режми. Как сказано выше, Taskbar может запускать приложения в Freeform только если их запускать остановленными.\n\nВы можете развернуть окна нажатием на место, не занятым приложениями.  Это расширит окно на весь экран не переходя в полноэкранный режим.\n\n<b>Включение полного режима Freeform</b>\n\nЕсли у вас есть доступ к компьютеру с установленным Android SDK, вы можете включить поддержку используя adb shell:\n\nsettings put global enable_freeform_support 1\n\nПерезагрузите устройство и новая кнопка появится в окне недавних приложений для переключения режима Freeform определенному приложению.</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_dialog_message">To configure your device for desktop mode, please follow these steps:\n\n&#8226; Go to Developer Options\n\n&#8226; Scroll to the bottom and turn on \"Enable freeform windows\" and \"Force desktop mode\"\n\n&#8226; Set Taskbar as your default home app\n\n&#8226; Reboot your device\n\nFor more information and troubleshooting steps, click the (?) icon in the upper-right hand corner of the screen.</string>

    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_message_2">Once granted, the additional settings will be enabled when a display is connected.</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_pref_description">Use Taskbar to launch windowed apps on an external display, similar to Samsung DeX and other solutions.\n\nRequires either a USB-to-HDMI adapter, or a lapdock, and a device that supports video output.</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_pref_description">Click here for info on how to enable</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_help"><b>About desktop mode</b>\n\nTaskbar supports Android 10\'s built-in desktop mode functionality. You can connect your compatible Android 10+ device to an external display and run apps in resizable windows, with Taskbar\'s interface running on your external display and your existing launcher still running on your phone.\n\nDesktop mode requires a USB-to-HDMI adapter (or a lapdock), and a compatible device that supports video output. Additionally, certain settings require granting a special permission via adb.\n\nTo get started, tick the checkbox for \"Desktop mode.\" The app will guide you through the one-time setup process, which involves:\n\n&#8226; Enabling the \"Enable freeform windows\" and \"Force desktop mode\" developer options\n\n&#8226; Setting Taskbar as your default home app\n\n&#8226; Rebooting your device\n\n<b>Why does Taskbar need to be set as my default home app for desktop mode to work?</b>\n\nBy design, Android will only allow the default home app to act as the launcher for desktop mode on external displays.  If the currently set home app does not specifically declare support for desktop mode, then Android will fall back to using a predefined desktop mode interface.\n\nTaskbar is able to work around this limitation by acting as a shim between the system and your preferred launcher.  When Taskbar is set as your home app, when the home button is pressed it will immediately start the launcher you prefer to use on your phone, then quit.  This launcher is set within Taskbar\'s desktop mode preferences by clicking on the \"Primary launcher\" setting.\n\nThere are a couple of caveats to keep in mind when Taskbar is set as your home app:\n\n&#8226; Most app shortcuts (seen when long-pressing an app icon) will no longer be available to your primary launcher.  This is due to an Android system limitation where only the default home app has access to app shortcuts that are dynamically created.\n\n&#8226; Certain animations that occur when pressing the home button may look incorrect.  This is due to the aforementioned behavior where Taskbar acts as a shim when the home button is pressed.\n\n<b>How do I know if my device supports video output to external displays?</b>\n\nThe folks over at NexDock maintain a list of Android 10+ smartphones that are compatible with USB-to-HDMI video output (this list excludes Samsung and Huawei devices).  You can view this list here: http://nexdock.com/lapdock-for-smartphones\n\nGoogle Pixel devices, notably, do not include support for video output.  However, you can still use Taskbar with standard screen mirroring on these devices, either via Chromecast or by using a DisplayLink dock coupled with the DisplayLink Presenter app.\n\n<b>Should I use Taskbar\'s desktop mode over my device\'s built-in desktop mode solution, such as Samsung DeX?</b>\n\nSome OEMs, such as Samsung and Huawei, have implemented their own version of desktop mode, building on top of Google\'s implementation in Android 10.  These solutions have better integration with the rest of the system and are generally more polished overall.  There is no need to switch over to Taskbar if you\'re happy with your device\'s existing desktop mode solution.\n\nTaskbar\'s desktop mode support is geared towards devices that don\'t offer a built-in desktop mode solution beyond what Google ships as stock with Android 10.\n\n<b>I can\'t find the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options &#8211; can I still use desktop mode?</b>\n\nSome devices (such as the ASUS ROG Phone 2) do not have the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options. You will need to enable that option manually via adb. To do so, connect your device to a computer with the Android SDK installed, and run the following command (one-line):\n\nadb shell settings put global force_desktop_mode_on_external_displays 1\n\n<b>What should I do if something goes wrong or my launcher / apps behave funky when desktop mode is enabled?</b>\n\nPlease follow these steps to disable desktop mode in case anything goes wrong:\n\n&#8226; Pull down the quick settings drawer, then enter system settings via the gear icon\n\n&#8226; Navigate to developer options and disable \"Enable freeform support\" and \"Force desktop mode\"\n\n&#8226; Reboot your device</string>

    <string name="tb_see_whats_new">See what\'s new in this release</string>
    <string name="tb_action_view">View</string>

    <string name="tb_error_creating_widget">Error creating widget</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast_alt">Start desktop mode first before enabling the input method fix</string>

<string name="tb_freeform_dialog_title">Serbestlik desteğini aktif et</string>
<string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_message">"Serbest biçim modu hakkında

Taskbar, Android 7.0+ (Nougat) da serbest biçimli yüzer pencerelerde uygulamaları başlatmanızı sağlar. Kök erişimi gerekli değildir. Android 8.0+ (Oreo) ayrıca bir adb kabuk komutuyla desteklenir.

Cihazınızı serbest biçimli modda başlatmak için yapılandırmak için aşağıdaki adımları uygulamanız yeterlidir:

1. Taskbar uygulamasında \"Serbest biçimli pencere desteği\" kutusunu işaretleyin.

2. Cihazınızda uygun ayarları etkinleştirmek için açılır pencerede görünen talimatları izleyin (tek seferlik kurulum)

3. Cihazınızın son uygulamalar sayfasına gidin ve tüm son uygulamaları temizleyin *

4. Serbest biçimli bir pencerede başlatmak için Taskbar kullanarak bir uygulama seçin

* En iyi sonuçlar için, özellikle Android 8.0+ sürümünde, serbest biçimli modu kullanmadan önce cihazınızı yeniden başlatın.

Pencere boyutu hazır ayarları

Taskbar çeşitli hazır boyutlarda uygulama pencereleri başlatabilir. Bir uygulama simgesine uzun basarak veya sağ tıklayıp \"Yeni pencere\" yi seçerek bir pencere boyutu seçebilirsiniz.

Belirli bir uygulama için pencere boyutu ön ayarını kaydetmek için, önayarı uzun süre basılı tutun ve varsayılan olarak ayarlandığını gösteren bir onay işareti görünecektir. Taskbar, sonraki başlatılan lansmanlarda bu seçilen boyutu kullanarak uygulamayı başlatır.

Pencere boyutları Android 8.0+ üzerinde düzgün çalışmıyor. Ancak, uygulamayı başlatarak, hemen kapatarak ve Android in genel bakış menüsünden tekrar başlatarak bu konuda çalışabilirsiniz.

Sorun giderme

• Uygulamalar hala tam ekran başlatıyor

Zaten belleğe yüklenmiş bir uygulama başlatılırsa, Taskbar ön plana getirecektir. Uygulamayı serbest biçime çevirmek için yeni bir durumdan başlatılmalıdır.

Uygulamayı tekrarlardan hızlıca kaydırın ve ardından Taskbar kullanarak son uygulamalar sayfasından yeniden başlatın. Bu işe yaramazsa, uygulama simgesine uzun basın, \"Uygulama bilgisi\" ni seçin ve ardından uygulamayı durdurmaya zorlayın. Alternatif olarak, cihazınızı yeniden başlatabilirsiniz.

• En iyileştirilen uygulamalar bir pencereye geri yüklenemez

Bir uygulamanın başlık çubuğunda Büyüt düğmesine basarsanız, Android uygulamayı serbest biçimli moddan ve tam ekran moduna geçirir. Yukarıda belirtildiği gibi, Taskbar, yeni bir durumdan başlatılırsa, uygulamaları yalnızca serbest biçimli pencerelere açabilir.

Bunu düzeltmek için, uygulamanın pencere çerçevesinin hemen dışına hafifçe dokunarak veya tıklayarak pencereleri en üst düzeye çıkarabilirsiniz. Bu, boş alanı, boş alan penceresi içinde saklarken tüm boş alanı dolduracak şekilde genişletecektir.

Tam sistem düzeyinde serbest form penceresi desteğini etkinleştirme

Android SDK yüklü bir bilgisayara erişiminiz varsa, aşağıdaki komutu çalıştırarak sistem düzeyinde serbest form modunu destekleyebilirsiniz (tek satır):

adb shell settings put global enable_freeform_support 1

Yukarıdaki komutu çalıştırdıktan sonra cihazınızı yeniden başlatın ve belirli bir uygulama için serbest biçim moduna giriş / çıkış yapmak için cihazınızın son uygulamalar sayfasındaki uygulama girişleri için yeni bir düğme görünecektir. (Düğmenin Android 7.1.2 de çalışmadığını unutmayın)"</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_dialog_message">To configure your device for desktop mode, please follow these steps:\n\n&#8226; Go to Developer Options\n\n&#8226; Scroll to the bottom and turn on \"Enable freeform windows\" and \"Force desktop mode\"\n\n&#8226; Set Taskbar as your default home app\n\n&#8226; Reboot your device\n\nFor more information and troubleshooting steps, click the (?) icon in the upper-right hand corner of the screen.</string>
    <string name="tb_reboot_required_alt">Reboot your device to complete desktop mode setup</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_message_1">Taskbar can optionally use elevated permissions to improve your desktop mode experience. These permissions are granted by running an adb shell command.\n\nIf you wish to continue, connect your device to a computer with the Android SDK installed, and run the following command (one line):</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_message_2">Once granted, the additional settings will be enabled when a display is connected.</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_pref_description">Use Taskbar to launch windowed apps on an external display, similar to Samsung DeX and other solutions.\n\nRequires either a USB-to-HDMI adapter, or a lapdock, and a device that supports video output.</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_pref_description">Click here for info on how to enable</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_help"><b>About desktop mode</b>\n\nTaskbar supports Android 10\'s built-in desktop mode functionality. You can connect your compatible Android 10+ device to an external display and run apps in resizable windows, with Taskbar\'s interface running on your external display and your existing launcher still running on your phone.\n\nDesktop mode requires a USB-to-HDMI adapter (or a lapdock), and a compatible device that supports video output. Additionally, certain settings require granting a special permission via adb.\n\nTo get started, tick the checkbox for \"Desktop mode.\" The app will guide you through the one-time setup process, which involves:\n\n&#8226; Enabling the \"Enable freeform windows\" and \"Force desktop mode\" developer options\n\n&#8226; Setting Taskbar as your default home app\n\n&#8226; Rebooting your device\n\n<b>Why does Taskbar need to be set as my default home app for desktop mode to work?</b>\n\nBy design, Android will only allow the default home app to act as the launcher for desktop mode on external displays.  If the currently set home app does not specifically declare support for desktop mode, then Android will fall back to using a predefined desktop mode interface.\n\nTaskbar is able to work around this limitation by acting as a shim between the system and your preferred launcher.  When Taskbar is set as your home app, when the home button is pressed it will immediately start the launcher you prefer to use on your phone, then quit.  This launcher is set within Taskbar\'s desktop mode preferences by clicking on the \"Primary launcher\" setting.\n\nThere are a couple of caveats to keep in mind when Taskbar is set as your home app:\n\n&#8226; Most app shortcuts (seen when long-pressing an app icon) will no longer be available to your primary launcher.  This is due to an Android system limitation where only the default home app has access to app shortcuts that are dynamically created.\n\n&#8226; Certain animations that occur when pressing the home button may look incorrect.  This is due to the aforementioned behavior where Taskbar acts as a shim when the home button is pressed.\n\n<b>How do I know if my device supports video output to external displays?</b>\n\nThe folks over at NexDock maintain a list of Android 10+ smartphones that are compatible with USB-to-HDMI video output (this list excludes Samsung and Huawei devices).  You can view this list here: http://nexdock.com/lapdock-for-smartphones\n\nGoogle Pixel devices, notably, do not include support for video output.  However, you can still use Taskbar with standard screen mirroring on these devices, either via Chromecast or by using a DisplayLink dock coupled with the DisplayLink Presenter app.\n\n<b>Should I use Taskbar\'s desktop mode over my device\'s built-in desktop mode solution, such as Samsung DeX?</b>\n\nSome OEMs, such as Samsung and Huawei, have implemented their own version of desktop mode, building on top of Google\'s implementation in Android 10.  These solutions have better integration with the rest of the system and are generally more polished overall.  There is no need to switch over to Taskbar if you\'re happy with your device\'s existing desktop mode solution.\n\nTaskbar\'s desktop mode support is geared towards devices that don\'t offer a built-in desktop mode solution beyond what Google ships as stock with Android 10.\n\n<b>I can\'t find the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options &#8211; can I still use desktop mode?</b>\n\nSome devices (such as the ASUS ROG Phone 2) do not have the \"Force desktop mode\" toggle in developer options. You will need to enable that option manually via adb. To do so, connect your device to a computer with the Android SDK installed, and run the following command (one-line):\n\nadb shell settings put global force_desktop_mode_on_external_displays 1\n\n<b>What should I do if something goes wrong or my launcher / apps behave funky when desktop mode is enabled?</b>\n\nPlease follow these steps to disable desktop mode in case anything goes wrong:\n\n&#8226; Pull down the quick settings drawer, then enter system settings via the gear icon\n\n&#8226; Navigate to developer options and disable \"Enable freeform support\" and \"Force desktop mode\"\n\n&#8226; Reboot your device</string>

    <string name="tb_see_whats_new">See what\'s new in this release</string>
    <string name="tb_action_view">View</string>

    <string name="tb_error_creating_widget">Error creating widget</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_summary">Fixes visual glitches caused by input methods on Android 11+. Requires you to temporarily set Taskbar as your input method each time desktop mode starts.</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast">Open desktop mode settings to enable a fix for visual glitches caused by input methods</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast_alt">Start desktop mode first before enabling the input method fix</string>

    <string name="tb_rom">ROM</string>

    <string name="tb_override_freeform_unsupported">Force-enable freeform mode settings</string>
    <string name="tb_override_freeform_unsupported_description">This version of Android does not support freeform mode on displays that do not set their default windowing mode to freeform (such as the built-in device display).\n\nIf you believe this is in error, toggle this setting on to force-enable the \'Freeform mode\" settings page.</string>

    <string name="tb_freeform_dialog_message">为了 任务栏 能够在自由式窗口模式中启动应用，请遵循以下步骤:\n\n&#8226; 前往开发者选项\n\n&#8226; 滑动到底部并打开 \"%s\"\n\n&#8226; 重新开机</string>

    <string name="tb_pref_title_invisible_button">任务栏 收起时隐藏按钮</string>
    <string name="tb_pref_title_freeform_mode_help">自由式模式的帮助 &amp; 使用方法</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_title">帮助 &amp; 使用方法</string>
    <string name="tb_freeform_help_dialog_message"><b>关于自由式模式</b>\n\n任务栏 能让您在 Android 7.0 (Nougat) 上在自由式窗口中启动应用。无需 Root 权限。 Android 8.0 (Oreo) 也可以通过 adb shell 命令实现\n\n遵循以下步骤让您的设备可以在自由式模式中启动应用:\n\n<b>1.</b> 在任务栏应用中的勾选方框 \"自由式窗口支持\" \n\n<b>2.</b> 遵循弹窗中出现的指示来启用设备上适当的设置\n\n<b>3.</b> 前往您的设备的最近应用页面并清除所有最近应用\n\n<b>4.</b> 选择要使用任务栏在自由式窗口启动的应用\n\n<b>窗口大小预设</b>\n\n任务栏可在多个预设大小中启动应用窗口。您可以通过长按或者右击应用图标选择窗口大小，然后选择\"新窗口\"\n\n要保存特定应用的窗口大小预设，只需长按预设，出现勾号就表明已设为默认。任务栏 将会在下次启动时使用所选的大小启动应用。\n\n谨记: 窗口大小不能在 Android 8.0 上正常使用。然而，您可以通过启动应用，立即关闭然后从 Android 的概览菜单中再次启动暂时解决这个问题。\n\n<b>问题排查</b>\n\n<i>&#8226; 应用仍然以全屏模式启动</i>\n\n如果应用已经在内存中载入，任务栏 会将其置于前台运行。为了在自由式模式中启动应用，必须将其在全新状态下启动。\n\n从最近中清除应用，然后使用 任务栏 从最近应用页面将其重新启动。如果不可行，然后长按应用图标，选择 \"应用信息，\" 然后强制停止这个应用。\n\n<i>&#8226; 最大化的应用无法还原为窗口</i>\n\n如果您点按应用的标题栏上的最大化按钮，Android 会将应用移出自由式模式，而后进入全屏模式。如以上提到的，如果这些应用以全新的状态启动，任务栏 仅能在自由式窗口中启动应用。\n\n要修复这个问题，您可以通过在应用窗口画面外轻按或者点击即可最大化窗口。在保持自由式窗口工作空间的同时扩大窗口至所有可用空间。\n\n<b>启用完整的系统级自由式窗口支持</b>\n\n如果您有已安装 Android SDK 的电脑的访问权，您可以通过运行如下 adb shell 命令在系统层面下启用对自由式模式的支持:\n\nsettings put global enable_freeform_support 1\n\n运行以上命令后，重启您的设备，将会在设备的最近应用页面中出现一个新按钮可用于进入/退出所选应用的自由式模式。 (谨记: 这个按钮目前在 Android 7.1.2 上不可用)</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_dialog_title">启用桌面模式支持</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_dialog_message">要为您的设备配置桌面模式，请遵循以下步骤：\n\n&#8226; 前往 开发者选项\n\n&#8226; 滚动到底部并开启 \"允许可自由调整的窗口\" 和 \"强制使用桌面模式\"\n\n&#8226; 设定 任务栏 为您的默认主屏幕应用\n\n&#8226; 重启您的设备\n\n更多的信息和除错步骤，点击屏幕右上角的 (?) 图标。</string>
    <string name="tb_reboot_required_alt">重启您的设备来完成桌面模式设定</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_message_1">任务栏可以可选的使用赋予的权限来提升您的桌面模式体验。这些权限可以通过运行 adb shell 命令来赋予。\n\n如果您想要继续，请将您的设备与已安装 Android SDK 的电脑相连，并运行下述命令 (仅一行)：</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_message_2">一旦赋予权限，额外的设置将会在连接至外部显示屏时启用。</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_pref_description">使用 任务栏 来在外接显示屏时启动窗口化应用，类似 Samsung DeX 或其他方案。\n\n需要 USB-to-HDMI 转接器或者笔记本坞站，以及支持视频输出的手机设备。</string>
    <string name="tb_enable_additional_settings_pref_description">点击这里来了解如何启用的信息</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_help"><b>关于桌面模式</b>\n\n任务栏 支持 Android 10\'s 内置的桌面模式功能。您可以将您兼容的 Android 10+ 设备连接至外部显示屏来将应用以可改变大小的窗口形式运行，任务栏 的界面运行在您的外部显示屏上而您的现有启动器仍保持在您的手机上运行。\n\n桌面模式需要 USB-to-HDMI 转换器 (或者笔记本坞站)，和支持视频输出的兼容设备。除此之外，部分设置需要通过 adb 赋予特殊的权限。\n\n要开始，点开\"桌面模式\" 的勾选框，应用会指导您完成这仅需一次的设置流程，涉及包括：\n\n&#8226; 启用 \"允许可自由调整的窗口\" 和 \"强制使用桌面模式\" 开发者选项\n\n&#8226; 设定 任务栏 为您的默认主屏幕应用\n\n&#8226; 重启您的设备\n\n<b>为什么 任务栏 需要被设置为我的默认主屏幕应用才可使用桌面模式呢？</b>\n\n默认情况下，Android 仅允许默认主屏幕应用用于外接显示屏上的启动器。如果当前设定的主屏幕应用并为特别声明支持桌面模式，那么 Android 将回退至使用预定义的桌面模式界面。\n\n任务栏 可以扮演系统和您的偏好启动器之间的夹层来绕过这个限制。当 任务栏 被设置为您的主屏幕应用时，按下主屏幕按钮时它将立即启动您偏好在手机上使用的启动器，然后退出。这个启动器在 任务栏 的桌面模式首选项中点击 \"首要启动器\" 设置。\n\n有一些当设置 任务栏 为您主屏幕应用时的事宜需要说明：\n\n&#8226; 大多数应用快捷方式(长按应用图标时看到的)将无法在您的首要启动器下工作。这是由于 Android 系统的限制只有默认主屏幕应用才可以访问动态生成的应用快捷方式。\n\n&#8226; 部分按下主屏幕按钮时的动画效果可以看起来不正确。这是由于之前提及的 任务栏 扮演了按下主屏幕按钮夹层的缘故。\n\n<b>我如何知道我的设备是否支持视频输出至外部显示屏呢？</b>\n\nNexDock 的人们维护了一个兼容 USB-to-HDMI 视频输出的 Android 10+ 智能手机的列表(该列表排除了 Samsung 和 Huawei 设备)。您可以在此查看该列表：http://nexdock.com/lapdock-for-smartphones\n\n值得一提的是，Google Pixel 设备并不支持视频输出。但是，您仍可以以标准的视频镜像方式在这些设备使用 任务栏，无论是使用 Chromecast 或者 DisplayLink 坞站配合 DisplayLink Presenter 应用。\n\n<b>我是否应该使用 任务栏 的桌面模式而不是我设备内建的桌面模式方案，比如 Samsung DeX 呢？</b>\n\n部分厂商，比如 Samsung 和 Huawei，实现了自己的桌面模式，在 Google Android 10 的基础之上。这些方案跟系统的其他部分整合度更好且通常整体上打磨的更完备。如果您对您设备上已有的桌面模式方案感到满意那么没有必要切换到 任务栏 的方案。\n\n任务栏 的桌面模式面向那些并未在 Google 在 Android 10 下已有方案之上内建桌面模式方案的设备。\n\n<b>我在 开发者选项 中找不到 \"强制使用桌面模式\" 开关&#8211; 我是否仍然可以使用桌面模式？</b>\n\n某些设备 (比如 ASUS ROG Phone 2) 在 开发者选项 中没有 \"强制使用桌面模式\" 开关。您需要使用 adb 手动开启该选项。如果要这样做的话，将您的设备与已安装 Android SDK 的电脑相连，并运行下述命令 (仅一行)：\n\nadb shell settings put global force_desktop_mode_on_external_displays 1\n\n<b>如果在桌面模式启动后我的启动器出错了/应用变得奇怪了该怎么办？</b>\n\n一旦感觉任何不对，请遵循如下步骤来禁用桌面模式：\n\n&#8226; 下滑快捷设置抽屉，通过齿轮图标进入系统设置\n\n&#8226; 前往 开发者选项 并禁用 \"允许可自由调整的窗口\" 和 \"强制使用桌面模式\"\n\n&#8226; 重启您的设备。</string>

    <string name="tb_see_whats_new">查看本次发布有什么新内容</string>
    <string name="tb_action_view">查看</string>

    <string name="tb_error_creating_widget">创建小部件时出错</string>

    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix">桌面模式输入法修复</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_title">启用输入法修复</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_summary">修复 Android 11+ 上输入法引起的视觉问题。每次桌面模式启动时需要临时将 Taskbar 设为输入法。</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast">打开桌面模式设置以启用输入法视觉问题修复</string>
    <string name="tb_desktop_mode_ime_fix_toast_alt">请先启动桌面模式再启用输入法修复</string>

    <string name="tb_rom">ROM</string>

    <string name="tb_override_freeform_unsupported">强制启用自由窗口模式设置</string>
    <string name="tb_override_freeform_unsupported_description">此 Android 版本不支持在未将默认窗口模式设为自由窗口的显示器（如设备内置屏幕）上使用自由窗口模式。\n\n如果您认为此判断有误，请开启此设置以强制启用\"自由窗口模式\"设置页面。</string>

    <string name="tb_shizuku_successful">已通过 Shizuku 成功启用额外设置</string>

<PreferenceScreen xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android">

    <ListPreference
        android:defaultValue="@string/tb_pref_theme_default"
        android:entries="@array/tb_pref_theme_list"
        android:entryValues="@array/tb_pref_theme_list_values"
        android:key="theme"
        android:negativeButtonText="@null"
        android:positiveButtonText="@null"
        android:title="@string/tb_pref_title_theme"/>

    <Preference
        android:key="icon_pack_list"
        android:title="@string/tb_icon_pack" />

    <Preference
        android:key="background_tint_pref"
        android:title="@string/tb_pref_title_background_tint" />

    <Preference
        android:key="accent_color_pref"
        android:title="@string/tb_pref_title_accent_color" />

FILE: app\src\nonlib\java\com\farmerbb\taskbar\activity\PersistentShortcutLaunchActivity.java
============================================================

/* Copyright 2020 Braden Farmer
 *
 * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
 * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
 * You may obtain a copy of the License at
 *
 *     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
 *
 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
 * limitations under the License.
